---
title: "LRP51: Bayesian DAG of word-reading gain (t1 → t2)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}
This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model
evolves and further data are received.
:::

```{python}
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

pd.set_option("display.float_format", "{:.3f}".format)

HERE = Path(".")
with open(HERE / "config.json") as fp:
    config = json.load(fp)

with open(HERE / "diagnostics.json") as fp:
    diagnostics = json.load(fp)

trace = az.from_netcdf(HERE / "trace.nc")
summary = pd.read_csv(HERE / "posterior_summary.csv", index_col=0)
```

## Overview

LRP51 is the first Bayesian model in the project. It estimates word
reading progress between timepoints t1 and t2 (the `ewrswr` score at t2)
under an explicit directed graph of influences: age, receptive and
expressive vocabulary, letter-sound knowledge, intervention group, and
intervention attendance across the t1–t2 interval.

Each outcome node is modelled as beta-binomial with a node-specific item
total. Each edge contributes a linear coefficient to its child's
logit-scale linear predictor; most edges additionally carry an
HSGP-approximated 1-D Gaussian process capturing variation from the
central linear trend. The intervention-group edge enters as a single
binary contrast. Baseline (t1) measurements of each outcome enter as
offsets, giving a "gain conditional on baseline" interpretation.

This is an **exploratory model**. Data, priors, and per-test item
totals are preliminary.

## Configuration

### Nodes

```{python}
#| output: asis

rows = []
for n in config["dag"]["nodes"]:
    rows.append({
        "Node": n["name"],
        "Outcome (t2)": f"`{n['outcome_column']}`",
        "Likelihood": n["likelihood"],
        "N": n["n_trials"] if n["n_trials"] is not None else "—",
        "Parents": ", ".join(f"{p['name']} ({p['kind']})" for p in n["parents"]),
    })
print(pd.DataFrame(rows).to_markdown(index=False))
```

### Sampler settings

```{python}
#| output: asis

rc = config["run_config"]
rows = [
    ("Run preset", rc["name"]),
    ("Draws", rc["draws"]),
    ("Tune", rc["tune"]),
    ("Chains", rc["chains"]),
    ("Target accept", rc["target_accept"]),
    ("Observations", config["n_observations"]),
    ("Random seed", config["random_seed"]),
]
print(pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=["Setting", "Value"]).to_markdown(index=False))
```

## Diagnostics

```{python}
#| output: asis

rows = [
    ("Divergences", diagnostics["divergences"]),
    ("r_hat max", f"{diagnostics['r_hat_max']:.3f}"),
    ("ESS min", f"{diagnostics['ess_min']:.0f}"),
    ("Parameters", diagnostics["n_parameters"]),
]
print(pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=["Metric", "Value"]).to_markdown(index=False))
```

### Trace (intercepts, linear coefficients, HSGP amplitudes / length-scales)

![](trace.png)

### Energy

![](energy.png)

## Posterior summary

Selected fixed-effect coefficients (intercepts, linear slopes, GP
amplitudes / length-scales, beta-binomial concentrations, residual SDs):

```{python}
#| output: asis

mask = summary.index.str.startswith(("b0__", "beta__", "eta__", "log_ell__", "kappa__", "sigma__"))
display_cols = ["mean", "sd", "hdi_5%", "hdi_95%", "ess_bulk", "r_hat"]
cols_present = [c for c in display_cols if c in summary.columns]
print(summary.loc[mask, cols_present].to_markdown(floatfmt=".3f"))
```

## Posterior predictive checks

```{python}
#| output: asis

for n in config["dag"]["nodes"]:
    path = HERE / f"ppc_{n['name']}.png"
    if path.exists():
        print(f"### Node {n['name']} — {n['outcome_column']}\n")
        print(f"![](ppc_{n['name']}.png)\n")
```

## Notes

```{python}
#| output: asis
print(config.get("notes", "—"))
```

## Model identifier

```{python}
#| output: asis
print(f"- Model: **{config['model_id']}**")
print(f"- Pipeline: `{config['pipeline_cls']}`")
```
